assertions.title: OAuth session restore survives reload for the same client ID

steps.action: Open the dedicated authenticated validator session on the disposable validation sheet and run the documented same-session preflight.
steps.expected: Both Google probes return 200 in the same browser session before auth evaluation begins.
steps.observed: GET userinfo returned 200 with the local gcloud token and GET Sheets metadata returned 200 with a freshly refreshed Hermes Sheets token in the same agent-browser session.

steps.action: Bootstrap signed-in state for the current OAuth client ID in that same browser session and inspect the MariaDB drawer.
steps.expected: The sign-in gate clears and write-capable drawer controls are visible before reload.
steps.observed: The Continue gate disappeared, account chrome showed emilio3435@gmail.com, and the MariaDB drawer exposed editable status, follow-up, reply, and notes controls.

steps.action: Reload the page without changing the configured OAuth client ID.
steps.expected: The same-client stored session restores automatically and keeps write-capable state available.
steps.observed: After reload, the account menu stayed visible, Continue remained absent, localStorage and sessionStorage OAuth markers were still present, and reopening the same MariaDB drawer still showed editable controls.

evidence.consoleErrors: none after clearing logs and rechecking the settled post-reload state

frictions.description: The saved runtime bridge token file had gone stale and returned Sheets 401 during the first probe attempt.
frictions.resolution: Refreshed the local Hermes Google token via its existing refresh token and used that refreshed access token for the Sheets-side preflight/bootstrap path.

frictions.description: The saved browser state alone still landed on the real Google identifier handoff and silent GIS restore returned interaction_required.
frictions.resolution: Completed the rerun with the documented same-session browser preflight plus runtime auth injection in the dedicated validator session instead of relying on the popup handoff.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: VAL-SETTINGS-010 passed. Same-session preflight succeeded (userinfo 200, Sheets metadata 200), and reloading with the same OAuth client ID preserved signed-in, write-capable state.
